task_completion.description: Verify that a new style named 'river_labels' is created in the 'ne' workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer 'Styles' list or 'Edit Style' page, the style name is 'river_labels' and the workspace is set to 'ne'.

task_completion.description: Verify the SLD style uses a TextSymbolizer with the 'name' attribute.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SLD XML editor, there is a <TextSymbolizer> block containing a <Label> element with <ogc:PropertyName>name</ogc:PropertyName> (or similar syntax for the 'name' attribute).

task_completion.description: Verify the SLD style uses LinePlacement for the labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SLD XML editor, inside the <TextSymbolizer>, there is a <LabelPlacement> block containing a <LinePlacement> element.

task_completion.description: Verify the SLD style includes the VendorOptions 'group' and 'followLine'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SLD XML editor, inside the <TextSymbolizer>, there are <VendorOption name="group"> and <VendorOption name="followLine"> tags (typically set to 'true').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'river_labels' style is applied as the default style for the 'ne:ne_rivers' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer 'Edit Layer' page for the 'ne_rivers' layer, under the 'Publishing' tab, the 'Default Style' dropdown is set to 'river_labels' (or 'ne:river_labels').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GeoServer web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoServer admin web interface being navigated and interacted with, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or external text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the SLD was edited within the GeoServer style editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SLD XML code is visible inside the GeoServer 'Edit Style' text area, not just pasted into a standalone file.

integrity.description: Verify the layer style assignment was done via the GeoServer layer publishing interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Layer' page for 'ne_rivers' is visible with the 'Publishing' tab selected and the style dropdown modified.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected style name is 'river_labels' in the 'ne' workspace. The style must be applied as the default style to the 'ne:ne_rivers' layer. The SLD XML must include a <TextSymbolizer> using the 'name' attribute, a <LinePlacement> element, and two <VendorOption> elements: 'group' and 'followLine'.
